cards.back: A classification of formal grammar systems based on their descriptive power, ranging from regular languages to context-free languages and beyond
A type of grammar that can generate an infinite number of strings, using non-terminal symbols and production rules
A type of grammar that can only generate strings that match a specific regular expression, with a finite number of production rules
A specific form of context-free grammar that is easy to parse and analyze, where all production rules have the form AX → αX' or AX → α
A type of parser that can parse context-free grammars with a single look-ahead, and is particularly useful for left-to-right parsing
